competitions.title: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: The world's largest global pre-collegiate science competition for high school students.
competitions.fullDetails: Students conduct original research projects across various scientific disciplines. To qualify for the international fair, participants must first succeed at affiliated regional and state-level competitions. This competition is renowned for fostering advanced research skills, critical thinking, and scientific presentation abilities.
competitions.deadline: Varies by regional/state fair (typically Jan-Feb 2026 for first rounds)
competitions.prizes: Scholarships, global recognition, and awards totaling tens of thousands of dollars at the international level.
competitions.fee: Not explicitly stated (may vary by regional fair)

competitions.title: Cambridge Re:Think Essay Competition 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An international essay competition encouraging critical thinking and exploration of diverse, thought-provoking topics for secondary-school students.
competitions.fullDetails: This competition offers two age-based categories: a Junior Division for ages 11–13 and a Senior Division for ages 14–18. It aims to foster intellectual rigor, originality, and clarity by inviting participants to deeply engage with historical, current, and speculative future scenarios. Entry is free.
competitions.location: Online (submissions), University of Cambridge (award ceremony in the UK)
competitions.prizes: Awards, certificates, and recognition, with award recipients invited to the University of Cambridge campus.

competitions.title: 2026 International Youth Competition
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition inviting students to share their ideas and perspectives on vital social challenges through visual art, essays, or open submissions.
competitions.fullDetails: Open to students in primary, middle, and high school, as well as college, worldwide. Participants can enter individually or as teams, submitting works in categories like Visual Art, Essay, or an 'Open' category for other innovative ideas. Judging criteria include message, idea, originality, and delivery. All participants receive a 'Distinguished Achievement' certificate.
competitions.interest: Social Challenges, Visual Arts, Essay Writing, Open Innovation
competitions.eventDate: Winners announced by end of September 2026
competitions.prizes: 1st, 2nd, and 3rd place awards for each category and division; all participants receive a 'Distinguished Achievement' certificate.

competitions.title: World Science Olympiad 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An online international science competition for students held annually.
competitions.fullDetails: The online examination for International Private Candidates is scheduled for April 26, 2026, at 9 AM Singapore Time. For students registering via Singaporean and International schools, the competition window runs from April 1 to April 30, 2026. A minimum of 10 students are required for school registrations.
competitions.deadline: Varies (registration via schools typically precedes event date)

competitions.title: International STEM Olympiad 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An online international STEM competition for students from grades 1 to 12, culminating in an onsite Grand Final in Italy.
competitions.fullDetails: The Olympiad covers Math, Science, Technology, and Engineering, with questions designed around STEM education principles. It includes online Qualification Exams (February 1, 2026), Final Exams (February 15, 2026), and Best of the Bests Exams (March 1, 2026). The STEM Grand Final will be an onsite event in Rome, Italy.
competitions.eventDate: Qualification Exam: February 1, 2026; Final Exam: February 15, 2026; Best of the Bests Exams: March 1, 2026; STEM Grand Final: July 2-8, 2026
competitions.rounds: Multiple (Qualification, Final, Best of the Bests, Grand Final)

competitions.title: Conrad Challenge 2026-2027
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition for high school students to develop innovative solutions to global challenges with an entrepreneurial focus.
competitions.fullDetails: Teams of up to five students, guided by a teacher or mentor, develop solutions in categories like aerospace, energy, and health. The challenge emphasizes both STEM innovation and entrepreneurship, guiding students through brainstorming and feedback phases, with finalists presenting at an Innovation Summit. The 2026-2027 round is anticipated to open in August 2026.
competitions.deadline: Not yet announced (expected to open August 2026)
competitions.eventDate: Innovation Summit for the 2025-2026 round is in April 2026; 2026-2027 dates to be announced after August opening.
competitions.prizes: Focuses on developing impactful solutions and providing mentorship.

competitions.title: FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: A major international competition where high school students design, build, and program industrial-sized robots to play a complex field game.
competitions.fullDetails: Teams typically have six weeks to complete their robot based on a newly revealed game challenge, with the 2026 season theme being 'REBUILT.' Beyond engineering, FRC teaches critical skills such as fundraising, project management, marketing, and teamwork. Regional and district events run through April, culminating in the FIRST Championship, which hosts over 600 teams.
competitions.deadline: Season kicked off January 10, 2026; regional/district deadlines vary.
competitions.eventDate: Regional/district events through April 2026; FIRST Championship (typically April)
competitions.location: Various regional/district locations, FIRST Championship in Houston, USA
competitions.prizes: Provides significant experience, recognition, and scholarship opportunities.
competitions.fee: Not specified (team participation implies costs for materials/registration)

competitions.title: SOF International Science Olympiad (ISO) 2026-27
competitions.shortDesc: A prestigious international competition challenging students in science and reasoning, with multiple levels of exams.
competitions.fullDetails: The ISO is open to students from classes 1 to 12. Level 1 exams are scheduled for October 30, November 19, and December 3, 2026. The competition aims to encourage students to showcase their talent and improve their knowledge in science. Applications must be submitted through schools at least 30 days before the exam date.
competitions.deadline: Approximately 30 days before selected exam date (e.g., September 30, 2026 for Oct 30 exam)
competitions.prizes: Cash awards, medals, and certificates for top three international rank holders; zonal and school-level recognition.
competitions.rounds: Two levels (Level 1, then Level 2 for top 5% of participants)

competitions.title: International Foundation Science Olympiad (IFSO) 2026-27
competitions.shortDesc: An online science Olympiad for students in classes 1 to 10, focusing on Physics, Chemistry, and Biology.
competitions.fullDetails: The IFSO is a 60-minute online multiple-choice question exam designed to encourage deeper scientific understanding and application of concepts beyond textbook knowledge. It aims to nurture scientific thinking, inquiry, and expose students to current developments in science and technology. Parental supervision is required during the online exam.
competitions.deadline: Not explicitly stated (check official website for 2026-27 schedule)
competitions.eventDate: Timings: 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM IST (specific dates not detailed for 2026-27 in snippets)
competitions.prizes: Not specified in snippets, but focuses on skill development and preparation for competitive exams.

competitions.title: National Student Leadership Conference (NSLC) 2026 Programs
competitions.shortDesc: A series of leadership and career exploration programs for outstanding middle and high school students at various university campuses.
competitions.fullDetails: NSLC offers diverse programs focusing on fields like Aerospace, Business, Engineering, Government & Law, and Medicine & Health Care. These immersive experiences are hosted at prestigious universities such as Duke University and UCLA, providing students with opportunities for leadership development and exposure to specific career paths.
competitions.deadline: Rolling (registration opens early, programs run throughout summer)
competitions.location: Various US university campuses (e.g., Duke University, UCLA)
competitions.prizes: Certificate of completion, leadership development, college and career insights.

dailyInspiration.title: The 13-Year-Old Prodigy Who Rewrote Kyrgyzstan's Math Olympiad History
dailyInspiration.story: Arlan Sayat, a 13-year-old from Kyrgyzstan, made history in July 2025 by winning a bronze medal at the International Mathematical Olympiad, the first ever for his country. Despite his young age, Arlan had been solving university-level math problems since nine, often training in challenging conditions. His remarkable achievement, against competitors years older, not only secured a national milestone but also shattered a long-held belief that such global accolades were unattainable for students from Kyrgyzstan.
dailyInspiration.takeaway: The ceiling everyone accepted was never really there.
